p2p3_frt_breaches.summary: Ongoing P2 ticket (Activity Manager Activities) opened Mon 18 May; SRE office-hours response took 4.6 hours against 2-hour target, ticket remains open.

Double bonus transfer in Galabet holiday promotion — 280 members received duplicate bonuses; some users got bonuses from two campaigns on a single deposit. Partner requested root cause analysis and a fix recommendation.

Stanleybet bonus events returning HTTP 400 — ~900 requests from Comtrade's iCore failed posting to the integration API. Root cause: incorrect product type in bonus event payloads. Partner was advised to fix their product type and implement a retry mechanism.

true_p1_incidents.name: Issues with segments having singularity features due to crm-api errors
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Partners were unable to create, update, or clone segments using singularity features with specific operators in crm-api versions 2.61.26 and 2.61.27, causing crm-api errors.

Impact: 88 out of 119 production brands (73%) were unable to create, update, or clone segments that use singularity features with operators such as "equals", "contains", or "not contains". Multiclass features were unaffected.

Cause: A bug introduced by PR #2250 in crm-api versions 2.61.26–2.61.27 caused errors when segments used singularity features with certain operators.

Resolution: The backend fix was merged and deployed to all impacted brands (platform v2.61.30). Partner communications sent. No customer action required.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Duplicate bonuses have been granted to H2Bet players on a recurring basis whenever BetConstruct returns an HTTP 503 error after already having credited the bonus internally. Fast Track's retry logic then reissues the request, producing a second grant for the same bonus event. This has been an ongoing, unresolved issue for at least 24 weeks.

Impact: H2Bet has experienced recurring bonus leakage of roughly 300-500 extra grants per week across both FreeSpins and Cash bonuses, sustained over 24-plus weeks. The exposure continues on a weekly basis as of the most recent monitoring check in mid-June 2026, with no confirmed reduction.

Cause: BetConstruct's operator API can return a 503 error after a bonus has, in fact, already been credited to the player, which is non-standard behavior for the API. Fast Track's system interprets the 503 as a failure and retries the request, resulting in the bonus being granted a second time. The root cause lies with BetConstruct's API and has not been fixed on their side.

Actions Taken: Fast Track deployed enhanced logging and monitoring that lets the team detect and validate duplicate-grant events within minutes rather than hours. A proposed workaround of treating 503 responses as success (PR #122) was rejected by H2Bet over traceability concerns and remains closed and unmerged; an observability-only change (PR #123) has merged but does not alter retry behavior; a further change (PR #124) is approved but scoped narrowly and does not address the 503-after-credit duplication. No technical fix has been deployed, the root cause remains open, and the incident stays in Monitoring status while Fast Track pursues direct engagement with BetConstruct and awaits H2Bet's approval of a mitigation.

true_p1_incidents.name: Spinbet Investigation
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Spinbet's SendGrid account was breached, requiring immediate containment that disrupted email sending for both Spinbet and Razed. The compromised SendGrid credentials had to be removed to mitigate the security risk. Because the credentials were shared, the containment action affected both brands simultaneously.

Impact: Outgoing email sending was disrupted for both Spinbet and Razed after the shared SendGrid credentials were disabled. Both environments were impacted because they used the same SendGrid key, and additional follow-up was required to rotate external-facing API keys. No active security threat was identified within Fast Track itself.

Cause: The root cause was a breach of Spinbet's SendGrid account, classified as partner-induced. Sharing the same SendGrid credentials across Spinbet and Razed meant containment of the breach forced email outages in both. Internal investigation across Cloudflare logs, Wiz, and database logs found no suspicious activity within Fast Track infrastructure.

Actions Taken: The affected environments were locked, email distribution was scaled down, and the compromised SendGrid credentials were removed to contain the breach. Fast Track then began rotating relevant external-facing API keys, sharing new credentials securely via 1Password, and working with the partner to set up an alternative email provider for both Spinbet and Razed. Ongoing email-restoration work continues in a separate incident channel, and this incident was closed from a security perspective on 9 June 2026.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : Lottogo Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: After enabling the crm-api-enforce-endpoint-permissions feature flag in production, partners (notably Lottogo and Coinbar) reported CRM access issues including missing email template versions and searches by ID returning no results.

Impact: Affected partners were unable to access or retrieve certain CRM data as expected, disrupting workflows like viewing email templates and managing live lifecycles. Reports suggested the issue affected multiple brands, though it wasn't confirmed as universal.

Causes: The rollout introduced stricter CRM API endpoint permissions that didn't account for some brands using special/custom user roles. Those roles were not included in the initial Unleash exclusion segment, so access to certain endpoints was unintentionally blocked when the flag became active.

Steps to resolve: Responders mitigated by adding the affected brand ID(s) to the appropriate Unleash segment and restarting the crm-api pods, then disabled the feature flag as a rollback to restore prior permission behavior. The partner confirmed functionality was restored, and follow-up work was noted to validate missed roles/brands and update the segment before attempting another rollout.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : Galabet Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: The 'My Rewards' button failed to appear for some Galabet accounts due to a frontend race condition, preventing affected users from accessing the rewards section of the site.

Impact: Some Galabet player and internal accounts could not access the 'My Rewards' section during affected sessions, though reward events were still processed and remained visible inside Fast Track. The issue has since been fully resolved and users can access rewards as expected.

Cause: A race condition in BetConstruct's frontend integration caused FasttrackCrm.init() to run before the CRM SDK was fully loaded, so the Rewards button silently failed to initialise and never appeared. The same pattern had previously been seen on Padisahbet.

Actions Taken: A platform-level fix was released in the FastTrack loader to ensure initialisation only happens after the SDK is ready, removing the dependency on partner-side workarounds. The fix is now live in production and tested across multiple brands. Galabet confirmed the issue was resolved on 12 June 2026 and the incident has been closed.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : Razed Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Customers in the Razed Production environment were blocked from launching or editing CRM lifecycles. The frontend was filtering out required segments because they had been assigned the wrong category when cloned. This prevented lifecycle launches and edits across the brand.

Impact: Razed Production customers were unable to launch or edit CRM lifecycles, which blocked them from managing campaigns until the fix was deployed. A global SQL check confirmed no other brand was affected. Business operations on Razed were degraded for the duration of the incident, but no other brands required forced remediation.

Cause: A bug in crm-api caused segments to be assigned the wrong category when lifecycles were cloned. The frontend then filtered out those mis-categorised segments, breaking the launch and edit flows.

Actions Taken: The affected segments were identified and manually corrected, and a fix was deployed to crm-api so that cloned lifecycles receive the correct segment category. A global SQL check confirmed the issue was isolated to Razed Production, so no forced rollout was needed and the fix will reach other brands via the normal stable release process. The partner confirmed resolution at 6:08pm CEST and the incident is now Closed.

true_p1_incidents.name: KAFKA incident on EU1 Cluster
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Kafka queues across multiple brands on the EU1 cluster were unable to process or deliver messages due to authentication failures, leading to widespread service outages across roughly 15 brands for about 80 minutes.

Impact: Kafka queues and related services for at least 15 brands — including Betlive, Crocobet, Roobet, Star-Casino, Campeon-Omega, JackpotCRM, Pressenter, Sisutech, Badhombre, Demo, Reactivaction, Reefspins, Rhino, SBTSolutions, TheIslandGame, and Yarrow — stopped processing or delivering millions of messages for about 80 minutes. Seven production environments were locked for deployments to prevent recurrence; production deployments for those environments have since been manually restored and confirmed healthy.

Cause: At 14:11 UTC a change to the 'kafka-acl-external-user' Terraform module (PR #397) altered how MSK SCRAM secrets are associated, resulting in the removal of secrets for all brands except Yarrow. This caused widespread authentication failures across the shared EU1 Kafka cluster.

Actions Taken: At 15:26 UTC the missing secrets were re-associated, restoring user access to the Kafka cluster. Services were restarted and message processing recovered. Deployment freezes were put in place for the affected environments and have since been lifted after manual verification. A fix for the Terraform configuration is being prepared to prevent recurrence; the incident is in Documenting status pending the post-mortem.

true_p1_incidents.name: Fable 5 No Longer Available
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Fable 5 was disabled, causing all AI services to go offline. After mitigation, service was restored but with degraded performance and minor errors.

Impact: All AI-powered services went offline for all brands. Partial service was restored using an Opus-based model, but response times are slower and some previously fixed prompt errors have returned.

Cause: Fable 5 was disabled by Anthropic due to US Government restrictions.

Actions Taken: Switched production to an Opus-based model to restore service. A phased deployment of a fix is underway and being validated on a subset of brands, with evals running to confirm stability. Coinbar, who reported the issue, is being updated.

true_p1_incidents.name: Overloaded Errors from Anthropic
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Anthropic returned 529 overloaded errors and at times delivered garbled, incoherent AI responses, causing FT AI to fail or respond incorrectly for partners across multiple brands.

Impact: 19 error requests in the prior 24 hours disrupted FT AI for partners across multiple brands. The most impacted brand was Gamius (4 errors), with Gembet, Reactivation, Kenhowells, Shuffle, Weindonesia, Onetwoplay and Betzilo also affected. Some responses were not delivered, others were jumbled but passed system checks and were still surfaced to users.

Cause: A recent code change required the AI assistant to format responses in a very rigid way which had been needed for Fable but was not compatible with the Anthropic model. The rigid formatting requirement caused Anthropic to occasionally return garbled output that still passed validation. Anthropic's servers were also under load and returning API 529 errors, compounding the user-visible impact.

Actions Taken: The rigid formatting requirement for Anthropic responses was removed (PR #378) and affected brands were migrated to AWS Bedrock, which restored coherent and stable AI behaviour across all brands. The fix is fully rolled out. The incident is now in the Documenting phase while the team completes the post-incident write-up; no further user-facing remediation is pending.

true_p1_incidents.name: Upgaming Platform Issues
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Multiple Upgaming platform services stopped processing messages because consumer pods got stuck on unprocessable 'poison' messages at the head of RabbitMQ queues, halting CRM integration, email distribution, and bonus distribution for several brands.

Impact: Customers of Ginjabet, Rolletto, Betfast, and Jackbit experienced delays or failures in CRM integration, email distribution, and bonus distribution actions. At peak, Rolletto had over 2.5 million unprocessed messages and all affected brands saw message processing halted for an extended period. Platform services have since been restored across all four brands.

Cause: Repeated operator/proxy API errors (HTTP 500/404) generated 'poison' messages at the head of RabbitMQ queues. These caused consumer pods for several Upgaming platform services to crash-loop, preventing any subsequent valid messages from being processed until the bad messages were cleared.

Actions Taken: All affected partners were notified and kept informed throughout the incident. Forwarders were scaled down to prevent further message build-up, then restored as queues were cleared. Rolletto's bonus queue was cleared, their RDS was temporarily upgraded to address performance issues, and Betfast's bonus queue was released per their requests. Ginjabet's previously blocked queues have now been resolved and the incident is closed.

true_p1_incidents.name: Memory DB Betconstruct Cluster production-274-gen over 90%
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: MemoryDB on the production-274-gen cluster exceeded 90% memory usage, causing the scheduled-actions-manager service to halt and message queues to grow for multiple brands.

Impact: Scheduled actions and campaign messages stopped processing across at least 19 brands from around 03:30 UTC. Automated notifications, bonuses, and scheduled messages were delayed for all affected brands, with large backlogs building up — over 1.2 million messages queued for Seu Bet and nearly 384,000 for Segurobet.

Cause: A surge of scheduled actions from Seubet (two activities each targeting over 2 million users) queued more than 5 million actions at once, consuming ~78 GB and pushing the shared MemoryDB cluster above 90%, which triggered the scheduled-actions-manager into a shutdown/restart loop.

Actions Taken: Upgraded MemoryDB to db.r6gd.4xlarge then db.r6gd.8xlarge to add capacity, allowing the queue to drain and service to restore. Seubet advised to stagger large sendouts by 30-45 minutes; alert thresholds under review for earlier warning.

p1_frt_breaches.summary: Internal Spinbet login outage reported to critical-support at 02:50 UTC (overnight, outside Malta office hours); SRE first response logged 79 min later (04:09 UTC) when the on-call SRE picked up the ticket — the breach was after-hours pickup, not an in-shift delay.

p2p3_frt_breaches.summary: Betpipo P3 'Report a Problem' ticket opened 26 Jun 11:08 CEST and routed first to Partner Support; the SRE office-hours first response exceeded the 2-hour P2/P3 SLA by a wide margin (~19 office-hours) as the ticket sat snoozed awaiting SRE triage — an ownership/routing gap rather than an in-shift delay.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident: iBet AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Users on the iBet brand and associated teams were unable to log in to Fast Track. The login failure blocked partner operators from reaching the CRM until the configuration was corrected.

Impact: All users on the iBet brand and related teams lost access to Fast Track for roughly 15 minutes between 07:10 and 07:27 UTC on 25 June. Other previously-flagged brands were confirmed unaffected by this specific cause, and customer confirmation was received once access was restored.

Cause: The configuration flag AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED had been enabled, which caused the WorkOS authentication layer to reject legitimate logins for iBet. The setting was incompatible with iBet's deployment posture and produced the auth-blocking behaviour the partner reported.

Actions Taken: Joachim Farrugia set AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED to false at 09:15 CEST (07:15 UTC), which immediately restored access for all impacted iBet users. The partner confirmed login was working and all previously affected brands were verified operational; the incident was closed the same morning.

true_p1_incidents.name: CLI Authentication Issue
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Fleet-wide CLI authentication failures stopped users from logging in and using CLI tooling, and the FT AI assistant could not operate because its authentication tokens were being invalidated. The failure spanned core CLI tools across the brand fleet.

Impact: Authentication failed for crm-cli, dashboard-cli and rewards-admin and for the FT AI assistant across up to 15 brands; some users were logged out of the CRM after interacting with FT AI. Most other CRM functions kept working. By 25 June roughly 75% of brands had the fix deployed, with brands such as Brazilbet, Softgenius and Shuffle delayed by configuration issues.

Cause: A recent crm-api security update bound admin tokens to a specific login IP. When the AI assistant or CLI sandboxes connected from a different cloud IP their tokens were invalidated; the IP-binding rule did not account for HMAC-signed requests, causing widespread auth failures.

Actions Taken: Engineering deployed crm-api fix PR #2412 to skip IP binding for HMAC-signed requests, rolling it out brand-by-brand, and temporarily disabled the IP-binding rule on some brands as an interim measure. Remaining per-brand configuration issues (e.g. Wiwi Casino environment variables) were corrected, affected partners were notified as fixes landed, and the incident was closed on 26 June once the fleet was on the fixed version.

true_p1_incidents.name: WorkOS 401 Unauthorized Issue
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: WorkOS authentication failures and a separate CRM-API pod problem prevented users from logging into Fast Track across several brands, producing repeated 401 Unauthorized errors during login.

Impact: Users from IBet, OneCasino, Meridianbet, Roobet, and Sway Gaming lost login access at various times between 09:10 and 12:30 CEST on 24 June. Betamina experienced a related but separate outage caused by CRM-API pod failures during the same window.

Cause: A misconfiguration in the WorkOS integration — AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED left on — rejected valid tokens and returned 401s across multiple brands. Betamina's parallel loss of login came from unhealthy crm-api pods rather than the WorkOS setting.

Actions Taken: We set AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED to false and redeployed crm-api, restoring login for IBet, Meridianbet, Roobet, OneCasino, and Sway Gaming. Betamina was resolved by fixing its crm-api deployment. Partner confirmation was received for all impacted brands and the incident was closed on 30 June after a monitoring window.

true_p1_incidents.name: Partner Login Failure — IP Binding
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: A partner was unable to log in to Fast Track because the AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED authentication configuration rejected valid logins. The failure was part of the same security-release rollout tracked under INC-10673.

Impact: The partner lost CRM access from 18:34 UTC on 24 June until the configuration was corrected around half an hour later. The partner confirmed resolution at 21:05 CEST the same evening.

Cause: The IP-binding security setting (AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED) was enabled on the partner's environment and blocked legitimate logins — the same root cause as the wider authentication rollout incidents that week.

Actions Taken: We set AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED to false, which immediately restored login functionality. The partner confirmed resolution and the incident was closed within about 30 minutes of being reported.

true_p1_incidents.name: FTCRM Access Outage — Reverted Auth Mitigations
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Customers were unable to access FTCRM after failing deployments reverted mitigations that had been applied for the ongoing authentication rollout incident.

Impact: Affected customers, including Meridianbet, could not access FTCRM or have queries resolved from 08:16 UTC on 25 June until access was restored at 10:28 CEST.

Cause: Failing deployments caused the previously applied authentication mitigations to be reverted, reintroducing the login failure from the security-release rollout.

Actions Taken: We resolved the deployment issues and restored access for affected customers by 10:28 CEST, then kept the Meridianbet system under monitoring. The incident was closed the same morning.

true_p1_incidents.name: CRM Login Issues - All Brands
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: A CRM API outage starting at 12:08pm CEST on June 26 caused partners across all brands to lose access to CRM logins, with automated email and SMS services failing. Backoffice systems, Dashboards, and Data Studio became unavailable.

Impact: From 12:08pm CEST on June 26, all partners across all brands could not log in to the CRM, and both production environments were affected. Automated email and SMS notifications were not sent, resulting in a surge in support tickets. Backoffice, Dashboards, and Data Studio were unavailable for most brands until deployments completed. No data loss occurred, and the core CRM engine continued processing activities as usual.

Cause: The CRM API became unavailable, leading to failures in dependent services such as email and SMS distribution. Error logs confirmed failed attempts to retrieve email templates and communication profiles from the CRM API.

Actions Taken: The team confirmed the CRM API outage and began restoring affected services. Services were restarted, restoring backoffice access for most brands by 14:10pm CEST on June 26. A new platform version (PV 2.62.39) was deployed in batches to all brands, with locked and custom environments handled individually. As of June 28, all unlocked brands have Dashboards/Data Studio restored; verification and communication for each environment were tracked using a deployment sheet. The incident has since been closed after all remaining locked/custom environments were verified.

true_p1_incidents.name: JackpotCRM — Slots Reporting Fields Zeroed
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: JackpotCRM's 'Total Bet Amount Slots' reporting field showed zero for all players across the 7, 14 and 30-day windows, blocking campaign segmentation.

Impact: No players received the planned campaign — segment evaluation returned no valid targets for all JackpotCRM users during the affected period, preventing targeted customer communications.

Cause: An environment version created on 9 June from base version 0.0.50 instead of the intended 0.0.67 reverted the ds calculator configuration for slots measures, so the player activity calculator ran against a fallback config without them.

Actions Taken: We rebuilt the environment from the correct base version, re-applied the required configuration, invalidated the broken versions and manually re-ran the player activity calculator, repopulating the affected fields. Segment evaluation was verified returning correct players and the incident was closed on 29 June.

p1_theme.label: AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED
p1_theme.summary: 5 of the week's 7 True P1s trace to the AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED security-release rollout rejecting legitimate logins (WorkOS, CLI/HMAC, iBet and partner-brand auth, plus reverted mitigations). Part of the 24 Jun–1 Jul security-release incident wave.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : Segurobet Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: SDT activities on Segurobet production were not being processed, which cascaded into delayed activity SMS delivery.

Impact: Some brands' SDT-driven activity SMS were held up as the Activity Manager queues backed up, degrading customer engagement flows for roughly two hours until the queues drained at 11:37pm CEST.

Cause: Sustained RDS CPU saturation on the Segurobet production database (95–99% CPU) caused the Activity Manager (SDT and fire) queues to back up while writes were blocked at the database layer.

Actions Taken: The Segurobet RDS instance was scaled from r6g.large through xlarge and 2xlarge to 4xlarge. Activity Manager fire consumer settings (prefetch, batch size) were adjusted and fire pod limits increased, while the conversion service was reduced to a single pod to ease load. Queues drained and activity processing caught up by 11:37pm CEST; the incident has now been closed.

true_p1_incidents.name: Multiple partners unable to login
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Multiple partner environments were unable to log in, with users hitting login loops and 404s across brands including Winspirit, Roobet, Crocobet, Betlive and several others.

Impact: Users on affected partner environments could not authenticate into the CRM. Some brands saw related service-specific access issues (Fusion API, rewards API, failed actions API) until their environments were redeployed and stale caches were bypassed.

Cause: An analytics-config change triggered long-running MySQL table rebuilds and locks that broke logins, while a recent kafka-acl-external-user module version bump was causing intermittent deployment failures on top of that. Residual issues on some clients were caused by stale browser-cached content.

Actions Taken: The analytics-config change was reverted and impacted brands were redeployed to restore access. The kafka-acl-external-user module version was rolled back, returning deployments to green. Partners were advised to hard-refresh, use incognito, or a cache-busting query string to work around stale cached logins. Residual service-specific failures were split into dedicated incidents and the parent P1 was closed once partner login access was confirmed stable.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: H2Bet and Seubet users were unable to access the rewards section, with a significant spike in UNAUTHENTICATED errors seen from 4pm CEST.

Impact: End users on H2Bet and Seubet could not authenticate against the rewards backend for several hours, blocking rewards access across multiple brands.

Cause: An invalid JWT secret was set in the rewards backend configuration for both brands, causing authentication errors on every token verification.

Actions Taken: We updated the JWT secret directly in the cluster for both brands using the correct value from 1Password. Error graphs dropped to zero on UNAUTHENTICATED after 3am CEST, and customer confirmation followed; incident is closed.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : Atomo Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: The rewards button was missing from at least one Atomo customer's page, blocking access to the rewards feature.

Impact: Affected Atomo customers could not see or access the rewards UI, cutting them off from expected rewards functionality until the fix was applied.

Cause: A misconfiguration in the rewards backend meant the end-user JWT secret was not set correctly, so authentication of the rewards call failed and the button did not render.

Actions Taken: We updated the rewards backend configuration to set the correct end-user JWT secret; the rewards button reappeared for affected customers and the incident was closed within roughly 90 minutes of report.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : BrazilBet Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: BrazilBet customers could not log in to the website and the rewards icon was missing, blocking authentication and rewards features.

Impact: BrazilBet customers were unable to log in or see the rewards icon between roughly 7:30pm and 7:50pm CEST on 30 June, resulting in failed logins and a rewards feature outage for the duration.

Cause: A misconfiguration in the BrazilBet environment variables caused a mismatch in the JWT secret key naming between 'fusion_api' and 'fusion-api' in the vault, so downstream services could not verify user tokens.

Actions Taken: The team manually updated the JWT token configuration for both the fusion API and rewards-backend to use the correct key from the vault, ensuring both keys were aligned. Service was restored and BrazilBet was running normally as of 7:54pm CEST; the incident has since been closed.

true_p1_incidents.name: CRM users getting logged out when clicking FT AI
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: CRM users were logged out immediately when clicking the FT AI button and could get stuck in a sign-in / sign-out loop, blocking them from working.

Impact: CRM operators across multiple brands including We88, Corestar, Starcasino, Mystake, Leaderbet and Zunado were repeatedly logged out and unable to use FT AI, disrupting normal operator workflows until each brand received the fix.

Cause: A misconfiguration of the JWT secret between the crm-api and FT AI services caused a mismatch in the authentication tokens, so sessions were invalidated whenever a user opened FT AI.

Actions Taken: A fix was implemented to route the correct JWT secret to both crm-api and FT AI. Deployments were rolled out to the affected brands to apply the fix once the deployment-system issues were cleared, restoring access. The incident has since been closed.

true_p1_incidents.name: Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Shortlinks and unsubscribe links for multiple brands were redirecting users to a Forbidden page instead of resolving normally.

Impact: Users of Supabets, Softgenius, Betathome, Merkurxtip, Stanleybet-romania, and Estelarbet could not open shortlinks or unsubscribe links, seeing a forbidden error instead.

Cause: A Cloudflare-origin check introduced for shortlink and unsubscribe requests was incorrectly rejecting valid brand traffic; a separate file-serving change that blocked deleted images had also been left in place and contributed to the issue.

Actions Taken: The team removed the Cloudflare-origin check for the affected brand paths and merged the fix, reverted the file-serving change for deleted images, cut a new stable platform version, and confirmed links were working again for Estelarbet and the reported templates. The incident remains in Monitoring while the team watches for recurrence across the other affected brands.

p1_theme.label: SECURITY_RELEASE_FALLOUT
p1_theme.summary: 4 of 7 True P1s were fallout from the 24 Jun – 1 Jul security release — three failure modes, all silent: detected by partners, not monitoring.

themes.label: JWT · end-user secret
themes.summary: Vault migration auto-created wrong end-user JWT secrets — login & Rewards down on 4 brands, 401 spikes on ~16 more.
themes.detail: Vault migration's create-if-not-found logic auto-generated mismatched end-user JWT secrets (fusion_api vs fusion-api). Login & Rewards down on BrazilBet, Atomo, H2Bet, Seubet; 401 rates up to 63% across ~16 further brands. Fixed, but secret persistence in Vault unconfirmed.

themes.label: JWT · FT AI rotation
themes.summary: crm-api and FT AI left on different secrets — FT AI clicks invalidated sessions, logout loops on 20+ brands.
themes.detail: crm-api and the FT AI services ended up on different JWT secrets after rotation, so any FT AI interaction invalidated the user's session — logout loops across 20+ brands including We88, Corestar, Starcasino, Mystake, Leaderbet, Zunado.

themes.label: Session IP binding
themes.summary: New enforcement killed sessions on IP change — Betpipo & Galabet force-logouts persisted after JWT fixes.
themes.detail: New session-IP-binding enforcement invalidated sessions whenever requests arrived from a different IP than login (VPN/proxy/dynamic-IP users). Betpipo & Galabet kept force-logging users out even after the JWT fixes; mitigated by disabling AUTH_IP_BINDING_ENABLED on both brands — a security-control rollback. Permanent fix pending.

p2p3_frt_breaches.summary: SRE first response took ~17.6 office hours (past the 2-hour P2/P3 SLA) after Partner Support escalated a P3 ticket from Momentum (IG Tech) about a blank/empty Test User page; SRE (Giancarlo Laferla) ultimately determined it was expected behavior due to a missing Testing:Users:Manage permission, not a bug.

true_p1_incidents.name: [P1] Intercom Incident : Mystake Production
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: Singularity time and RTD services were processing player activity extremely slowly on 8 Jul for brands deployed that day; Mystake raised a P1 as activity ingestion and downstream lifecycle triggers were delayed.

Impact: Player activity processing was delayed across 8 brands (Gamdom, Mystake, Vaycasino, Alpha-Iota/QPone, Betlive, Norabahis, Orisbet, Leader-bet), delaying deposit-driven triggers, campaigns and SMS/email touchpoints while singularity RabbitMQ queues backed up. Detection lagged roughly 4 hours because a slow-acking consumer kept the queues alive, so no zero-consumer alert fired.

Cause: A Helm templating defect in the singularity-model-engine chart (rtd-configmap.yaml) evaluated the encryption conditional before the Argo Vault plugin substituted enable_pii_encryption, so all GCP KMS entries (including GCP_PROJECT_ID) were omitted from the ConfigMap and services failed with "failed to initialise GCP key service: GCP project ID is required". It hit every brand deployed with the Project Zero release-candidate that day; the underlying platform incident is tracked as INC-12761.

Actions Taken: Argo-deployments commit 69ad447 set encryption explicitly in the Argo manifests so the chart reads the GCP values from Vault, the blocked Argo sync was terminated, and the ConfigMap was force-synced, with pods recovering and queues resuming at approximately 18:12 CEST on 8 Jul. Comms were sent to affected brands that had not already raised the issue, and a long-term Helm chart fix is tracked in singularity-model-engine PR #454. The incident is fully resolved and closed.

true_p1_incidents.name: MemoryDB 274 Reached 90% Threshold
true_p1_incidents.summary:
Problem: MemoryDB 274 reached 90% memory utilization and became stuck mid-upgrade, stalling scheduled-actions processing across the platform.

Impact: Scheduled actions were delayed for 21 brands while the instance could not process queued work, affecting shard/host scaling and time-based campaign lifecycles for those brands.

Cause: AWS determined the scaling stall was caused by a service-level issue in which shards and new hosts became stuck during host creation; root cause sits with AWS's MemoryDB provisioning path rather than Fast Track configuration.

Actions Taken: The team completed the scale-up of MemoryDB 274 to db.r6gd.4xlarge, validated key-count consistency post-cutover (~5.43M keys), and re-enabled scheduled actions. A support ticket was raised with AWS and escalated to their MemoryDB team; the incident remains in the Documenting (post-incident) stage pending AWS's official RCA and preventive guidance.
